title: Winding Number
summary: The winding number is the integer degree assigned to an isolated bad point by the normalized map from an enclosing sphere in momentum space to the target sphere.
assumptions: The enclosing surface avoids other singularities.
regime: Topological classification of isolated point nodes.
scope: Lecture One point-node charge in degree and Berry-flux language.

source_anchors.notes: The same integer appears as a degree and as a flux.

representation: Integer-valued invariant attached to an isolated point node.
failure_modes: If the enclosed region contains multiple nodes, the value is only the total charge.
retrieval_hints: Use for questions about node charge, degree, or chirality summation.
